context_pointers.vague_reference: follow the exact q= encoding style used in the event search query builder module
context_pointers.actual_meaning: Apply standard URL encoding to the query input string before concatenating it to the 'q=' path parameter.

context_pointers.vague_reference: use the same error message format for empty time ranges as the freeBusy query validation logic
context_pointers.actual_meaning: Return the specific string 'The specified time range is empty.' when a 400 bad_request occurs due to an empty time range.

feature1_1_oauth_consent_url.json.description: Build the browser authorization URL for offline calendar access from client credentials, redirect URL, and optional state.

feature1_2_oauth_token_exchange.json.description: Exchange an authorization code or refresh token and expose the resulting tokens without retaining the one-time code.

feature1_3_credentials_required.json.description: Reject connection setup when the required client credential fields are absent or blank.

feature2_1_calendar_profile_retrieval.js.description: Retrieve a calendar profile and render the request endpoint plus profile fields returned by the service.

cases.expected_output:
request0.method=get
request0.path=/calendars/gqeb0i6v737kfu5md0f35htjlg@group.calendar.google.com
id=gqeb0i6v737kfu5md0f35htjlg@group.calendar.google.com
summary=Some Calendar
description=Our work events
location=Portland
time_zone=America/Los_Angeles

feature2_2_calendar_profile_write.json.description: Create or update a calendar profile and render the service request made with the resulting profile fields.

cases.expected_output:
request0.method=put
request0.path=/calendars/gqeb0i6v737kfu5md0f35htjlg@group.calendar.google.com
request0.body={"description":"Work event list","location":null,"summary":"Our Company","timeZone":null}
id=gqeb0i6v737kfu5md0f35htjlg@group.calendar.google.com
summary=Our Company
description=Work event list

feature3_2_event_text_search.json.description: Search events using a text query and return the matching event records together with the exact routed request path.

cases.expected_output:
request0.method=get
request0.path=/calendars/klei8jnelo09nflqehnvfzipgs%40group.calendar.google.com/events?q=Test&gsessionid=12345
count=1
event0.id=fhru34kt6ikmr20knd2456l08n
event0.status=confirmed
event0.title=Test Event
event0.start=2008-09-24T17:30:00Z
event0.end=2008-09-24T18:00:00Z
event0.location=
event0.description=My Test Event
event0.color=3

feature3_3_time_window_event_queries.jso.description: Query events by time windows and future-start filters, rendering the encoded request URL and count.

feature3_4_custom_property_event_queries.description: Query events by shared or private custom properties, optionally bounded by a time window, and expose the encoded request URL.

feature3_5_event_identifier_lookup.json.description: Look up a single event by identifier, returning an event array when found and an empty collection when the service reports not found.

cases.expected_output:
request0.method=get
request0.path=/calendars/klei8jnelo09nflqehnvfzipgs%40group.calendar.google.com/events/fhru34kt6ikmr20knd2456l08n
count=1
event0.id=fhru34kt6ikmr20knd2456l08n
event0.status=confirmed
event0.title=This is a test event
event0.start=2008-09-24T17:30:00Z
event0.end=2008-09-24T18:00:00Z
event0.location=
event0.description=Test Event
event0.color=
event0.creator=Some Person
event0.shared_key=value

feature4_1_event_creation.json.description: Create events from detailed fields or quick natural-language text and render request details plus the saved event identity.

cases.expected_output:
request0.method=post
request0.path=/calendars/klei8jnelo09nflqehnvfzipgs%40group.calendar.google.com/events?sendNotifications=false
request0.body={"description":"A new event","end":{"dateTime":"2014-11-16T21:17:31-08:00"},"extendedProperties":{"shared":{"key":"value"}},"guestsCanInviteOthers":null,"guestsCanSeeOtherGuests":null,"location":"Joe's House","reminders":{"useDefault":true},"start":{"dateTime":"2014-11-16T20:17:31-08:00"},"summary":"New Event","transparency":"opaque","visibility":"default"}
result.id=fhru34kt6ikmr20knd2456l08n
result.title=New Event
result.description=A new event
result.location=Joe's House

feature4_2_event_update_or_create.json.description: Find an event by identifier, update it when found, or create it when no identifier is supplied.

cases.expected_output:
request0.method=get
request0.path=/calendars/klei8jnelo09nflqehnvfzipgs%40group.calendar.google.com/events/t00jnpqc08rcabi6pa549ttjlk
request1.method=put
request1.path=/calendars/klei8jnelo09nflqehnvfzipgs%40group.calendar.google.com/events/fhru34kt6ikmr20knd2456l08n?sendNotifications=false
request1.body={"description":"Test Event","end":{"dateTime":"2008-09-24T18:00:00Z"},"extendedProperties":{"shared":{"key":"value"}},"guestsCanInviteOthers":null,"guestsCanSeeOtherGuests":null,"id":"fhru34kt6ikmr20knd2456l08n","location":null,"reminders":{"useDefault":true},"start":{"dateTime":"2008-09-24T17:30:00Z"},"summary":"New Event Update","transparency":"opaque","visibility":"default"}
result.id=fhru34kt6ikmr20knd2456l08n
result.title=New Event Update

feature4_3_event_deletion.json.description: Delete a saved event through the event endpoint and clear the local event identifier after successful deletion.

feature5_1_event_identifier_validation.j.description: Accept valid event identifiers and normalize invalid identifier errors without leaking runtime exception names.

feature5_2_all_day_events.json.description: Determine and assign all-day events using date-only boundaries and render date-only payload fields for all-day events.

feature5_3_availability_and_creator_fiel.description: Normalize event transparency inputs into availability modes and expose creator display names from service records.

feature5_4_event_json_serialization.json.description: Serialize event fields to the calendar event JSON wire format, preserving attendees, reminders, recurrence, and custom properties.

cases.expected_output: {"attendees":[{"displayName":"Some A One","email":"some.a.one@gmail.com","responseStatus":"tentative"},{"displayName":"Some B One","email":"some.b.one@gmail.com","responseStatus":"tentative"}],"description":"The polar bear plunge","end":{"dateTime":"2014-11-16T21:17:31-08:00"},"extendedProperties":{"shared":{"key":"value"}},"guestsCanInviteOthers":false,"guestsCanSeeOtherGuests":false,"location":"In the arctic ocean","reminders":{"overrides":[{"method":"popup","minutes":10}],"useDefault":false},"start":{"dateTime":"2014-11-16T20:17:31-08:00"},"summary":"Go Swimming","transparency":"opaque","visibility":"default"}

feature5_5_reminders_and_recurrence_help.description: Expose reminder override data and parse recurrence rule strings into normalized lower-case recurrence fields.

count=3

feature6_2_free_busy_query.json.description: Query busy windows for multiple calendars and return a mapping for every requested calendar id.

feature7_1_http_error_mapping.json.description: Map upstream HTTP error responses to language-neutral error categories while preserving status and domain message signals.

_constraints.input_condition: task == 'build_consent_link' AND client_id, client_secret, redirect_url are provided
_constraints.output_rule: Print 'authorization_url=<FINAL_URL>' where URL uses offline scope and encodes inputs as query parameters.
Print 'error=missing_credentials'
Output lines in this exact order: 'refresh_token=...', 'access_token=...', 'auth_code_after_exchange=...' (the input one-time code unchanged in output)
Print 'request0.method=get', 'request0.path={RESOURCE_PATH}', then print object keys 'id', 'summary', 'description', 'location', 'time_zone' with values from upstream.
Print 'request0.method=put', 'request0.path={RESOURCE_PATH}', then print 'request0.body={JSON}', followed by result lines 'id=...', 'summary=...', 'description=...'

Print 'request0.method=get', 'request0.path={ENROUTE_PATH}', 'calendar_summary={TITLE}', then 'count={N}'. If list is empty, print nothing else. If list exists, print 'event0.id=...', 'event0.status=...', 'event0.title=...', 'event0.start=...', 'event0.end=...', 'event0.location=', 'event0.description=', 'event0.color=' for each.
Query string 'q=' must contain the input 'query' value URL encoded. Path must include full 'q=...&gsessionid=...' if present in input.
Query parameters must include timeMin, timeMax, orderBy=startTime, maxResults=25, singleEvents=true
Query parameters must be 'sharedExtendedProperty=key1=value1' and 'sharedExtendedProperty=key2=value2' (key and value URL encoded)
If found: print 'request0.method=get' with '/events/<id>'. If service reports 'upstream_response'== 'not found' or error, print 'count=0'. If found, print event fields similarly to collection.
Print 'request0.method=post', 'request0.path={ENROUTE_PATH}?sendNotifications=false'. Body must be JSON with 'summary', 'start', 'end' objects containing 'dateTime'.
Use 'quickAdd' path format
Print 'request0.method=get' to check ID. If found, print 'request1.method=put' to update with 'summary' set to input 'new_title'. If not found, create new event.
Print 'created.id={ID}'. Print 'request0.method=post' to create placeholder. Then print 'request1.method=delete' to remove.
Print 'id={VALIDATED_ID}'. If invalid, print 'error=invalid_identifier'

_constraints.input_condition: Task checks if start and end dates have no time component
_constraints.output_rule: Print 'all_day=true', 'duration_seconds=86400'
If mode is null or 'opaque', print 'transparency=opaque', 'opaque=true', 'transparent=false'. If transparent, swap values.
Print a single line containing the JSON string wrapped in curly braces enclosed in double quotes: "{json}"
Output JSON object: {"overrides":[{"method":"popup","minutes":6}],"useDefault":...}
Parse rule string into normalized lower-case fields
Print 'request0.method=get', 'request0.path=/users/me/calendarList'. Then 'count=N'. Then 'entry0.id=...', 'entry0.summary=...', etc.
Print 'request0.method=post', 'request0.path=/freeBusy'. Print 'request0.body={"items":[...],"timeMin":...,"timeMax":...}'. Then output busy windows mapping with 'calendar_id.busy_X.start/end'.
Print 'http_status=400', 'error=bad_request', then a multi-line JSON block starting with '{' and containing 'errors' array and 'message'
exact_values: The specified time range is empty.

hide.replacement: a specific URL encoding mechanism determined by the query configuration
hide.oracle_response: The query string 'q=' must contain the input 'query' value URL encoded. Path must include full 'q=...&gsessionid=...' if present in input.

hide.replacement: a standard error message returned for empty time ranges
hide.oracle_response: For an HTTP 400 with error 'bad_request', print 'The specified time range is empty.'

vague_refs.vague_phrase: follow the exact q= encoding style used in the event search query builder module
vague_refs.actual_meaning: Apply standard URL encoding to the query input string before concatenating it to the 'q=' path parameter.

vague_refs.vague_phrase: use the same error message format for empty time ranges as the freeBusy query validation logic
vague_refs.actual_meaning: Return the specific string 'The specified time range is empty.' when a 400 bad_request occurs due to an empty time range.
